message:
type: SURVEY
files: src/click/termui.py,src/click/_termui_impl.py
note: Adding process_priority param to click.edit() (termui.py 'edit()' function) and Editor class in _termui_impl.py: __init__ constructor (new process_priority attr) and edit_file() method (subprocess.Popen call needs priority applied, e.g. via preexec_fn/os.nice on Unix or creationflags/psutil on Windows).

type: SURVEY
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Adding multi-file support to click.edit(): renaming Editor.edit_file() to Editor.edit_files() (accepts iterable of filenames) in _termui_impl.py, and updating the edit() function signature/body in termui.py to accept str | Iterable[str] | None for filename param.

type: DRAFT
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py
region: Editor.edit_file -> edit_files() method
code: def edit_files(self, filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) -> None:
    import subprocess

    editor = self.get_editor()
    environ: dict[str, str] | None = None

    if self.env:
        environ = os.environ.copy()
        environ.update(self.env)

    exc_filenames = " ".join(f'"{filename}"' for filename in filenames)

    try:
        c = subprocess.Popen(
            f"{editor} {exc_filenames}",
            env=environ,
            shell=True,
        )
        exit_code = c.wait()
        if exit_code != 0:
            raise ClickException(
                _("{editor}: Editing failed").format(editor=editor)
            )
    except OSError as e:
        raise ClickException(
            _("{editor}: Editing failed: {e}").format(editor=editor, e=e)
        ) from e

note: Proposal for the multi-file rename (edit_file -> edit_files, joins quoted filenames). I have NOT included process_priority handling since I don't know your exact mechanism (preexec_fn/os.nice vs creationflags). Please send back a DRAFT with the full merged method including your priority code layered on top of this filenames-join approach, and I will adopt it verbatim.

type: DRAFT
files: src/click/termui.py
region: edit() function
code: def edit(
    text: t.AnyStr | None = None,
    editor: str | None = None,
    env: cabc.Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
    require_save: bool = True,
    extension: str = ".txt",
    filename: str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None = None,
    process_priority: str | None = None,
) -> t.AnyStr | None:
    r"""Edits the given text in the defined editor.  If an editor is given
    (should be the full path to the executable but the regular operating
    system search path is used for finding the executable) it overrides
    the detected editor.  Optionally, some environment variables can be
    used.  If the editor is closed without changes, `None` is returned.  In
    case a file is edited directly the return value is always `None` and
    `require_save` and `extension` are ignored.

    If the editor cannot be opened a :exc:`UsageError` is raised.

    Note for Windows: to simplify cross-platform usage, the newlines are
    automatically converted from POSIX to Windows and vice versa.  As such,
    the message here will have ``\n`` as newline markers.

    :param text: the text to edit.
    :param editor: optionally the editor to use.  Defaults to automatic
                   detection.
    :param env: environment variables to forward to the editor.
    :param require_save: if this is true, then not saving in the editor
                         will make the return value become `None`.
    :param extension: the extension to tell the editor about.  This defaults
                      to `.txt` but changing this might change syntax
                      highlighting.
    :param filename: if provided it will edit this file (or files, if given
                     an iterable of filenames) instead of the provided text
                     contents.  It will not use a temporary file as an
                     indirection in that case.
    :param process_priority: optionally set the CPU priority of the editor
                             process.  One of ``"low"``, ``"below_normal"``,
                             ``"normal"``, ``"above_normal"``, ``"high"``.
                             If not supported or setting it fails, the
                             editor is still launched at default priority.
    """
    from ._termui_impl import Editor

    ed = Editor(
        editor=editor,
        env=env,
        require_save=require_save,
        extension=extension,
        process_priority=process_priority,
    )

    if filename is None:
        return ed.edit(text)

    ed.edit_files(filename)
    return None
note: Merged: your filename str|Iterable[str] support + my process_priority passthrough to Editor().

type: DRAFT
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py
region: Editor.edit_file -> edit_files() method
code: def edit_files(self, filenames: str | cabc.Iterable[str]) -> None:
    import subprocess

    if isinstance(filenames, str):
        filenames = (filenames,)

    editor = self.get_editor()
    environ: dict[str, str] | None = None

    if self.env:
        environ = os.environ.copy()
        environ.update(self.env)

    exc_filenames = " ".join(f'"{filename}"' for filename in filenames)

    try:
        c = subprocess.Popen(
            f"{editor} {exc_filenames}",
            env=environ,
            shell=True,
            **self._popen_kwargs(),
        )
        exit_code = c.wait()
        if exit_code != 0:
            raise ClickException(
                _("{editor}: Editing failed").format(editor=editor)
            )
    except OSError as e:
        raise ClickException(
            _("{editor}: Editing failed: {e}").format(editor=editor, e=e)
        ) from e
note: Adopted your naming (exc_filenames, join style) verbatim. Added: (1) str-vs-iterable normalization at top -- needed because Editor.edit() and termui.edit() both may pass a single filename string, and your signature/body assumed an iterable already; (2) **self._popen_kwargs() at the Popen call -- this pulls process_priority handling from a helper method I'm adding solely in __init__/_popen_kwargs (not overlapping your code, safe for me to add independently). Please confirm this exact text back with AGREE.

type: AGREE
